Det var så mye Gud i den gamle mannens hjerte at han kunne se Ham overalt. Hvis du får Ham inn her inne, kan du se Ham der ute. Det stemmer. Han vil bruke øynene dine til å se gjennom. Du vil ikke se skader, farer, feil og ting; du vil bare se Gud, uansett hvor du er.

text: I remember standing there, the side of this tree, to finish the story of that before we go to another subject. Standing there beside the tree, I was just a praising God. I lifted up my hands and started crying. You'll probably call me a holy-roller anyhow, so you might as well get used to it now. I run around that tree just as hard as I could go, screaming at the top of my voice. Why, if somebody come there, they'd've thought somebody was there out of the insane institution, or the Salvation Army was out there in the woods. I was screaming and a running and a hollering as hard as I… I had to give vent to my feeling. God was all around me. There He was in the rainbow, there He was in the wolves, there He was in the elk herd, there He was everywhere. I said, "O Jehovah, oh, I love you!" And around, and around, around the tree I went again, just as hard as I could.
text_no: Jeg husker at jeg sto ved siden av dette treet, og jeg vil fullføre den historien før vi går over til et annet tema. Mens jeg sto ved treet, priste jeg Gud. Jeg løftet hendene og begynte å gråte. Du vil nok kalle meg en fanatiker uansett, så du kan like gjerne venne deg til det nå. Jeg løp rundt treet så fort jeg kunne, og skrek av full hals. Hadde noen kommet dit, ville de trodd at enten noen fra en sinnssykeanstalt eller Frelsesarmeen var i skogen. Jeg skrek, løp og ropte så hardt jeg kunne. Jeg måtte gi uttrykk for mine følelser. Gud var overalt rundt meg. Der var Han i regnbuen, i ulvene, i elgflokken - Han var overalt. Jeg sa, "Å, Jehova, å, jeg elsker Deg!" Og jeg løp rundt treet igjen, like raskt som jeg kunne.

text:
I looked out and I seen the sun setting, I said, "Oh, Your all-seeing eye, You watch over me. There You are hollering yonder in the wolf. There you are bugling in the elk. Oh, You're everywhere Jehovah."
And around and around that tree I went again. I couldn't help it, there was something in me, call to worship. I was in His Presence, in the greatest cathedral I ever sat in in my life: God's great cathedral. Hearing them pines come together, that whisper of the pines saying, "There's a land beyond the river, that they call the sweet forever." As it was singing like that, them pines a whispering. Oh, I thought, "That's right, Lord. Hallelujah!" I said. That great land somewhere. And I was a worshipping. And I stopped. I was just having a great big old-fashioned jubilee by myself. But I didn't care who heard me. I was worshipping God! So I stopped a few minutes. I thought, "Oh, how wonderful."
text_no:
Jeg så ut og så solen gå ned. Jeg sa: "Å, Ditt allseende øye, Du våker over meg. Der roper Du i ulven. Der trompeterer Du i elgen. Å, Du er overalt, Jehova."
Rundt og rundt det treet gikk jeg igjen. Jeg kunne ikke hjelpe det; noe inni meg kalte til tilbedelse. Jeg var i Hans nærvær, i det største katedral jeg noen gang hadde vært i: Guds store katedral. Hørte disse furutrærne hviske: "Det er et land bortenfor elven, som de kaller det søte evige." Som de sang slik, disse furutrærne hvisket. Å, jeg tenkte: "Det stemmer, Herre. Halleluja!" sa jeg. Det store landet et sted. Og jeg tilba. Jeg stoppet opp. Jeg hadde en stor, gammeldags jubel alene, men jeg brydde meg ikke om hvem som hørte meg. Jeg tilba Gud! Så stoppet jeg noen minutter og tenkte: "Å, hvor underbart."

text:
I begin to hear something, I looked over to my side and there sat a little old pine squirrel. I don't know whether you have them here or not. That's the noisiest thing in the woods. And there he was sitting up there, with that little tail curled up, going "Chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter. Chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter," like he was going to tear me to pieces. I thought, "Did I excite you little fellow? Didn't aim to." I said, "Oh, you felt like I did. Hallelujah! Hallelujah!" And I said, "Oh, how wonderful."
And he kept, "Chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter." His cotton-blue eyes looked down like that. I thought, "He's not watching me." And all at once I noticed a great big old eagle had been forced down in the storm, under this blow down, and he jumped up there, looked at me with them big gray eyes, looked back at that little pine squirrel. I thought, "Now God, You're in the wolf, You're in the elk, You're in the sunset, You're in the wind, You're in the tree, You're in the rainbow, why did You break this up for? Now what's in that eagle? What's that pine squirrel got to do with it? Me here worshipping You, and something like that happen."
text_no:
Jeg begynte å høre noe. Jeg så over til siden, og der satt en liten gammel fururygg. Jeg vet ikke om dere har dem her. Det er det mest bråkete dyret i skogen. Der satt den med halen krøllet opp, og skravlet: "Kjattr, kjattr, kjattr, kjattr, kjattr. Kjattr, kjattr, kjattr, kjattr, kjattr," som om den skulle rive meg i stykker. Jeg tenkte: "Skremte jeg deg, lille venn? Det var ikke meningen." Jeg sa: "Å, du følte det som jeg gjorde. Halleluja! Halleluja!" Og jeg sa: "Å, hvor vidunderlig."
Den fortsatte med: "Kjattr, kjattr, kjattr, kjattr." De bomullsblå øynene så ned. Jeg tenkte: "Den ser ikke på meg." Plutselig la jeg merke til en stor gammel ørn som hadde blitt tvunget ned av stormen, under et veltet tre. Ørnen hoppet opp og så på meg med de store grå øynene, og så tilbake på den lille fururyggen. Jeg tenkte: "Gud, Du er i ulven, Du er i elgen, Du er i solnedgangen, Du er i vinden, Du er i trærne, Du er i regnbuen; hvorfor avbrøt Du dette? Hva er det med den ørnen? Hva har denne fururyggen med det å gjøre? Jeg er her og tilber Deg, og så skjer noe slikt."

text: I looked at this big eagle, I thought, "Well, what makes him so brave?" I thought, "Say, fellow, are you scared?" I seen he wasn't scared. I thought, "There's God, right there." God's not a coward. And He can't use cowards. Afraid to testify of your healing, or your salvation, He can't use you, you're no good, see. He's got somebody that's got a backbone in them that'll stand up and say, "Right's right and wrong's wrong." That's the kind of people He can use. That's admired by anybody. That's admired among men by women. No matter how ugly she is, or what she might be, he'll admire her if she holds her place. Don't care what she is. That's right. It's admired among any, among soldiers, everywhere. It's among … in God's army, how that God admires. Like David, after His own heart.
text_no: Jeg så på denne store ørnen og tenkte: "Hva gjør ham så modig?" Jeg undret, "Er du redd?" Jeg så at han ikke var redd. Jeg tenkte: "Der er Gud, rett der." Gud er ingen feiging, og Han kan ikke bruke feiginger. Hvis du er redd for å vitne om din helbredelse eller frelse, kan Han ikke bruke deg; du er ubrukelig, skjønner du. Han trenger noen med ryggrad som tør å stå opp og si: "Rett er rett og galt er galt." Det er den typen mennesker Han kan bruke. Slikt beundres av alle. Det beundres blant menn og kvinner. Uansett hvor lite tiltrekkende hun måtte være, vil han beundre henne hvis hun står fast ved sine prinsipper. Det spiller ingen rolle hvordan hun ser ut. Det gjelder overalt, også i Guds hær. Gud beundrer slike mennesker, akkurat som David, som var etter Hans eget hjerte.

text: I seen this old eagle sitting there. I thought, "I admire you, but really, are you scared?" I thought, "You know here sits my rifle; I could shoot you." I wouldn't do it for nothing. So I said, "You know I could shoot you?" That didn't bother him. I thought, "I believe I'll just find out if he'll jump if he knows what that rifle is." I grabbed that rifle. When I grabbed the rifle, he looked at me like that, snapped them big eyes, and looked at me. But he wasn't worried about me; looked like that little chipmunk was getting on his nerves, that little pine squirrel. Get that little tail curled up, going, "Chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter." And I thought, "My, my, you're a noisy little fellow." And I thought, "Why ain't you scared? What makes you not scared?"
text_no: Jeg så en gammel ørn sitte der. Jeg tenkte, "Jeg beundrer deg, men er du virkelig redd?" Jeg tenkte, "Du vet at jeg har en rifle her; jeg kunne skyte deg." Selvfølgelig ville jeg aldri gjøre det. Så jeg sa, "Vet du at jeg kunne skyte deg?" Det brydde han seg ikke om. Jeg tenkte, "Jeg tror jeg skal se om han hopper hvis han vet hva den riflen er." Jeg grep riflen. Da jeg grep den, så han på meg med store øyne. Men han var ikke bekymret for meg; det virket som om den lille jordekornet plaget ham. Den lille halen krøllet seg opp mens den gikk, "Knakke, knakke, knakke, knakke, knakke, knakke." Jeg tenkte, "Oi, du er en støyende liten fyr." Og jeg undret meg, "Hvorfor er du ikke redd? Hva gjør at du ikke er redd?"

text:
Well, I happened to notice, he kept doing like this, you know, with his wings. "Ah," I said, "I see it," see. God give him two wings. That's God's provided way for him. He knowed before I could get that rifle to my shoulders, he'd be in that brush, see. He knowed where he was standing, so he wasn't scared.
And if God will give an eagle that much courage, with two wings to go to safety, what would He do to a man that's filled with the Holy Ghost? What ought that to do, as long as He's present? I thought, "That's right!"
So, after a while he got tired of looking at that little old pine squirrel … or, listening to him, so he just made one big jump, flopped his wings a couple times, he was out of the bushes, out into the mid air. And then I cried. He never flopped anymore, he never tried to flop his wings and get away; he just set his wings. And every time that air would come in, he just laid out and set his wings right in that air, it just lifted him up. I watched him. The air come in it lifted him up, lifted him up, until he was plumb out of sight, leaving that little old pine squirrel sitting there cursing and going on the way he was.
I thought, "That's it, Lord, not a jump, jump, here, run, run, here, join the Methodist, join the Baptist, come back to this and be baptized this way, that way, that isn't it. It's just setting your wings of faith. When the wings of the Holy Ghost comes in, ride away from it, on and on, on and on, plumb out of sight. Leaving this little old earth-bound doctrine here, "Chatter, chatter, chatter: days of miracles is past. Chatter, chatter, chatter: no such thing as divine healing. Chatter, chatter, chatter: you don't get the Holy Ghost."
Leave it, soar away, go on. Just set your wings, ride away. God sends the wind down, ride up on it. That's right. Get away from this earth-bound chatter, chatter, "This can't happen," and, "That can't happen." I said, "Lord, that's it."
text_no:
Jeg la merke til at ørnen stadig gjorde slik med vingene sine. "Ah," sa jeg, "jeg ser det." Gud ga ham to vinger. Det er Guds tilveiebrakte måte for ham. Han visste at før jeg kunne få riflen til skulderen, ville han være i buskene. Han visste hvor han stod, så han var ikke redd.
Om Gud gir en ørn så mye mot, med to vinger for å komme i sikkerhet, hva vil Han da gjøre for en person fylt med Den Hellige Ånd? Hva burde det gjøre, så lenge Han er til stede? Jeg tenkte, "Det er riktig!"
Etter en stund ble ørnen lei av å lytte til den lille furuskrikken og gjorde et stort hopp, flakset med vingene et par ganger og var ute av buskene, ut i luften. Og så begynte jeg å gråte. Han flakset ikke mer, prøvde ikke å slite seg bort; han bare satte vingene sine. Hver gang vinden kom, strakte han ut vingene og lot seg løfte høyere og høyere til han var helt ute av syne. Den lille furuskrikken satt der fortsatt og kjeftet.
Jeg tenkte, "Det er det, Herre. Ikke et hopp her og der, eller løping hit og dit, eller å gå frem og tilbake mellom konfesjonene. Det handler om å sette troens vinger. Når Den Hellige Ånds vind kommer, ri på den, fjernt fra alt. Legg jordbundne doktriner bak deg, 'Kjefting: miraklenes tid er forbi. Kjefting: det finnes ingen guddommelig helbredelse. Kjefting: du får ikke Den Hellige Ånd.'
Forlat det, stig til værs. Bare sett vingene og ri av gårde. Gud sender vinden ned, ri opp på den. Det stemmer. Kom deg bort fra dette jordbundne kjeftet, 'Dette kan ikke skje,' og, 'Det kan ikke skje.' Jeg sa, 'Herre, det er det.'"

text:
That's it, friends. God is in His universe. He speaks out to His people. You believe that? Now, we'll have to hurry, we can stay on that for hours.
God is in His Word. Do you believe that? Every word of God is a seed. Jesus said so. The Word is a seed. And what the seed is, it'll produce just exactly what the seed is.
In the Bible He was called Jehovah-jireh, Jehovah-rapha, and Jehovah our banner, Jehovah-manasseh, all those redemptive names, all that was promises, who Jehovah was. Every divine promise of God will come to pass if it's found in the right ground.
Here's what happens, here. Many times people, I notice them in the meeting, watching these tapes as they come back, through the lines of discernment. The people'll be sitting there look like they're a million miles away. They don't even be, they don't think of something… They're even thinking about healing. But it's not their intellects here working, it's their subconscious working. Your subconscious is your soul.
text_no:
Slik er det, venner. Gud er i universet. Han taler til sitt folk. Tror dere det? Nå må vi skynde oss, ellers kan vi bli værende i dette emnet i flere timer.
Gud er i sitt Ord. Tror dere det? Hvert Guds Ord er et frø. Jesus sa det. Ordet er et frø, og frøet vil produsere akkurat det det er.
I Bibelen ble Han kalt Jehova-jireh, Jehova-rapha, og Jehova vår banner, Jehova-manasseh, og alle de andre forløsningsnavnene. Alle var løfter om hvem Jehova var. Hvert guddommelig løfte fra Gud vil gå i oppfyllelse dersom det finner riktig jord.
Her er hva som skjer. Mange ganger legger jeg merke til folk i møtene, spesielt når jeg ser opptakene senere, gjennom linjene av skjelning. Folk ser ofte ut som de er millioner av mil unna. De tenker ikke engang på noe… De tenker ikke på helbredelse. Men det er ikke deres intellekt som er aktivt her, det er deres underbevissthet som arbeider. Din underbevissthet er din sjel.

text:
Why, not long ago, to show you how perfect God's Word is, the Bible said, "As a man thinketh in his heart," (Is that right?), "so is he." The old-time infidel used to say, "Now, isn't that crazy? Why, there's no mental faculties in a man's heart. It's a little pump that pumps blood. He can't think in his heart. He thinks in his head. So, the Bible is wrong," according to them.
Last year, along about January, last year … two years ago, it is now, I was in Chicago, and a great big headlines in the paper, that science has found… Listen to this: "Science has found in the human heart there's a little compartment that doesn't even have a blood cell in it. It isn't in the animal, it's only in the human." And they say, "It's the occupant of the soul." So, then a man does think from his heart, God was right! Now here, see, in here, you say, "Now let me see, yes, that says that, but I wonder if that was for me. Hmm! I just don't know whether that was or not." That's reasoning. You can reason with this.
text_no:
Ikke lenge siden, for å vise hvor perfekt Guds Ord er, sa Bibelen: "Som en mann tenker i sitt hjerte," (Er det riktig?), "så er han." De gammeldagse ateistene pleide å si: "Nå, er ikke det sprøtt? Hvorfor, det finnes ingen mentale evner i en manns hjerte. Det er en liten pumpe som pumper blod. Han kan ikke tenke i sitt hjerte. Han tenker med hodet. Så Bibelen tar feil," ifølge dem.
For to år siden, rundt januar, var jeg i Chicago, og en stor overskrift i avisen leste at vitenskapen har funnet... Hør på dette: "Vitenskapen har funnet at det i menneskehjertet er et lite område som ikke engang har blodceller. Det finnes ikke hos dyr, bare hos mennesker." Og de sier: "Dette er stedet for sjelen." Så, da tenker en mann faktisk fra sitt hjerte, Gud hadde rett! Her inne sier du kanskje: "La meg se, ja, det står der, men jeg lurer på om det gjelder meg. Hmm! Jeg vet bare ikke om det gjør det eller ikke." Det er resonnering. Du kan resonnere med dette.

text:
That's the reason preachers today, that don't believe in divine healing. It's salvation has never come down in their heart, it's only their head. They've went to a seminary, learned a whole lot of theology. You ought to forget it all and find Christ. How can you believe when there's nothing in you to believe with? It's only a mental theology.
Just recently we had a woman in Louisville, Kentucky, and she was around a church there, a pretty good girl. But a big fine church, a Methodist church. I always thought that the girl didn't have … wasn't right. But she married some old boy, there, was really a good Christian boy, full of the Holy Ghost. They went off, and after they'd got married… Her father was a deacon in that church, a fine man, Spirit-filled man.
text_no:
Det er grunnen til at forkynnere i dag, som ikke tror på guddommelig helbredelse, ikke har fått frelsen ned i hjertet. Den er kun i hodet deres. De har gått på et seminarium og lært mye teologi. Man burde glemme alt dette og finne Kristus. Hvordan kan du tro når det ikke finnes noe å tro med inni deg? Det er kun mental teologi.
Nylig hadde vi en kvinne fra Louisville, Kentucky. Hun var tilknyttet en menighet der og var en ganske bra jente. Men den store, fine menigheten, en metodistkirke… Jeg hadde alltid en følelse av at noe ikke stemte med jenta. Men hun giftet seg med en gutt som var en virkelig god, kristen gutt, fylt av Den Hellige Ånd. Etter de giftet seg… Hennes far var diakon i menigheten, en god mann, fylt av Ånden.

text:
And so, she wandered around out in the country, and first thing you know, they … some promotion, his job called him to go to another city. When he went to another city, then he took a job there. And she's a pianist, so she become the pianist in that church. And come to find out, that Methodist church wasn't very spiritual. And noticed that nearly all the women smoked cigarettes. Shame on you. Biggest fifth columnist move the world's ever had. Wish I had time to dwell on it. Preachers will do that though.
Look, I've got my opinion of women that smoke cigarettes. And I sure have. I had it when I was a boy and I've never changed it. Oh, that's the littlest thing you ever done. And it's the greatest harm to this nation and to motherhood, that's ever been done. Statistics shows that eight… I believe it's about eighty or ninety percent of the children that's born, if a mother nurses them and don't give them… Unless she gives them cow milk, she'll take nicotine poisoning, the baby will, and never live eighteen months. Talk about a fifth columnist.
Don't you worry about Russia coming over here and getting us. Russia ain't going to get us, it's our own rottenness that's getting us, that's what it is. It ain't the robin that pecks on the apple that hurts it, it's the worm at the core. That's exactly right. So, that's the other side, these preachers will tell you about that.
But now, that's what you need today, is a good old-fashioned house cleaning in the church! Right. Get back to a good old Saint Paul's revival and the Bible Holy Ghost again. Now wonder we're in the corruption that we are. Certainly, it is. Just feel the Holy Spirit, make you push it out anyhow.
text_no:
Så hun vandret rundt på landet, og før du vet ordet av det, fikk mannen hennes en forfremmelse som krevde at han flyttet til en annen by. Da han flyttet dit, fikk han en ny jobb. Hun er pianist, så hun ble pianist i den nye menigheten. Det viste seg at denne metodistmenigheten ikke var særlig åndelig. Det var tydelig at nesten alle kvinnene røyket sigaretter. Skam dere! Dette er den verste form for undergraving verden har sett. Skulle ønske jeg hadde tid til å utdype dette. Forkynnere gjør slikt.
Se, jeg har min mening om kvinner som røyker sigaretter, og den har jeg hatt siden jeg var gutt. Jeg har aldri endret den. Det er den minste dårligdommen du kan gjøre, men den skader nasjonen og morsrollen mer enn noe annet. Statistikken viser at hvis rundt åtti eller nitti prosent av barn som blir ammet får i seg kumelk eller ikke blir ammet, vil de få nikotinforgiftning og knapt leve i atten måneder. Snakk om undergraving!
Ikke bekymre deg for at Russland skal komme og ta oss. Russland vil ikke få oss; det er vår egen råttenhet som vil ta oss, det er det som er saken. Det er ikke fuglen som hakker på eplet som skader det, men ormen i kjernen. Det er helt riktig. Forkynnere vil fortelle deg om dette.
Det vi trenger i dag, er en god gammeldags husrens i menigheten! Riktig. Kom tilbake til en ekte Paulus-vekkelse og Den hellige ånds kraft fra Bibelen. Ikke rart vi er i den korrupsjonen vi er i. Det er sikkert. La Den Hellige Ånd fylle deg og rydde opp uansett.

text: Notice, then all this, this girl, she started smoking too. She begin to reason. "Why, if the other women could do it, they're well thought of, why can't I do it?" And begin to notice her neighbors, all of her sisters in the neighborhood, wear these little old scandalous clothes. And of course, you know, you have to wear them out when the men's coming in from the, you know, from the factories and things. You just have to get out in the yard. You don't appear before men like that … you don't appear before God like that, you're doing it to appear before men. And you mothers that let your kids do that, you ought to have a good old-fashioned time of posterior protoplasm stimulation added to you. That's exactly right.
text_no: Merk deg dette: Denne jenta begynte også å røyke. Hun begynte å resonnere. "Hvorfor, hvis de andre kvinnene kan gjøre det og de er vel ansett, hvorfor kan ikke jeg gjøre det?" Hun begynte å legge merke til naboene sine, alle søstrene i nabolaget, som gikk med disse små, skandaløse klærne. Selvfølgelig, du vet, du må bruke dem når mennene kommer hjem fra fabrikkene og lignende. Du må bare komme deg ut i hagen. Du viser deg ikke for Gud på den måten; du gjør det for å vise deg for menn. Og dere mødre som lar barna deres gjøre det, dere burde virkelig få en gammeldags stimulering av bakende protoplasma. Det er helt riktig.

text:
What we need today is some good old-fashioned mammy's that would… I said I got two girls, they may lay stretched out, one of them one time too, to get a suntan. They'll get a suntan, it won't be from that sun out there, it'll be with Mr. Branham's son with a barrel slat so hard that I could [unclear word] them. They'll get a suntan all right. That's right. Be my father's son doing it, too. Yes, sir. It's a shame how you let down the bars, you Methodist, Baptist, and Pentecostal too! Certainly. Used to be wrong in the church, but now, oh, the devil might've went out of fashion, but he didn't go out of business, I'll tell you that now. Hallelujah! I feel religious.
Let me tell you, brother, what we need today is a good old-fashioned taking the fire out of the furnace and put it on the altar again. Get it back out into the hearts of the people and get the revival started! If it was wrong then, it's wrong now. Right.
text_no:
Det vi trenger i dag, er noen gode gammeldagse mødre som kan... Jeg har to jenter, og de kan muligens legge seg ned for å få litt brunfarge. De får brunfarge, men den kommer ikke fra solen der ute; den kommer fra Herr Branham sin sønn med en trelist så hard at jeg kunne [uklart ord] dem. De vil få en brunfarge, det er sikkert. Det er min far som gjør det også. Ja, sir. Det er en skam hvordan dere har senket standardene, dere metodister, baptister og pinsevenner også! Absolutt. Det var en gang galt i menigheten, men nå... Åh, djevelen kan ha gått ut av moten, men Han er absolutt ikke ute av virksomhet, det kan jeg fortelle dere. Halleluja! Jeg føler meg religiøs.
La meg fortelle deg, Bror, det vi trenger i dag, er å ta ilden ut av ovnen og legge den på alteret igjen. Få den tilbake i hjertene til folket og få i gang en vekkelse! Hvis det var galt før, er det galt nå. Riktig.

text:
Notice, there, she begin to wear those little clothes. Her husband said, "Dear, I don't believe…"
"Now," she said, "Look, if Suzy, and Ella, and all of them can wear them, why can't I?"
See, right down in her soul it said, "That's wrong." Conscience will tell you that. But what did she do? She went to her intellect, to reason. "Well, now, if the others can do it, why can't I do it? They're well thought of, we're all Christians, they go to church. Why can't I do it, if they do it?"
text_no:
OBS, hun begynte å bære de små klærne. Mannen hennes sa: "Kjære, jeg tror ikke..."
Hun svarte: "Se nå her, hvis Suzy, Ella, og alle de andre kan gå med dem, hvorfor kan ikke jeg?"
Nede i sjelen hennes visste hun at det var galt. Samvittigheten fortalte henne det. Men hva gjorde hun? Hun brukte intellektet og resonneringen. "Vel, hvis de andre kan gjøre det, hvorfor kan ikke jeg? De er godt likt, vi er alle kristne, de går i menigheten. Hvorfor kan ikke jeg gjøre det, hvis de gjør det?"

text:
Now you see, the Bible commands us to cast down reasoning, see. That's where preachers don't believe in divine healing. That's where people don't believe in the supernatural. Only thing they got is intellectual reasoning. Listen, this soul in here is God. And God will agree with His Word every time. He'll not reason, He'll believe it. And when you believe it from your heart, the Bible said, "You'll have it." That's what it is, you're trying to make reason take it, when it's your heart that has to take it.
First thing you know, she began to… A little slicker moved in the neighborhood with a barrel of grease on his hair nearly. He begin to kiss her goodnight at the back fence, a little love affair. And the first thing you know, she left poor John, run off and married this little slicker. Didn't live together but a little while till he found one a little prettier than she was, so… She ought to have sense enough to know that. He run off and left her. And she married another one. Then he run off and left her, then she had a common-law husband.
text_no:
Bibelen befaler oss å kaste vrak på rasjonalisering. Det er derfor enkelte forkynnere ikke tror på guddommelig helbredelse, og hvorfor folk ikke tror på det overnaturlige. Deres eneste utgangspunkt er intellektuell rasjonalisering. Hør her, denne sjelen er Gud, og Gud vil alltid være enig med sitt Ord. Han vil ikke rasjonalisere, Han vil tro. Når du tror med hjertet, sier Bibelen at "du skal ha det." Problemet er at du prøver å bruke fornuft, mens det er hjertet som må ta det til seg.
En dag flyttet det inn en ung, sjarmerende mann i nabolaget, med håret dekket av blankt fett. Han begynte å kysse henne godnatt ved bakgjerdet, og et lite kjærlighetsforhold blomstret opp. Til slutt forlot hun stakkars John og giftet seg med denne unge mannen. Samlivet varte ikke lenge før han fant en penere kvinne og forlot henne. Hun burde ha forstått det. Hun giftet seg deretter på nytt, men også denne mannen forlot henne. Til slutt levde hun i et ekteskap etter vanlig rett.

text: But you know, the Bible said, "The soul that sinneth, that soul shall die." Death means "separate." She just kept getting away from it farther, farther, farther, and it getting away from her, see. The first time that she started smoking, she ought to've known. The soul says, "That's wrong." Reasoning said, "But the rest of them can do it, why can't I?" When she put on those little ungodly clothes, nature itself would tell her that's wrong. But she said, "If the rest of them do it, why can't I?" See where our American women's got to?
text_no: Men du vet, Bibelen sier: "Den sjel som synder, den skal dø." Døden betyr "å bli separert." Hun fortsatte bare å bevege seg lenger og lenger bort, mens det også ble fjernere fra henne. Første gang hun begynte å røyke, burde hun ha visst det. Sjelens stemme sier: "Det er feil." Fornuften sa: "Men de andre kan gjøre det, hvorfor kan ikke jeg?" Da hun tok på seg de små ugudelige klærne, ville naturen selv fortelle henne at det var galt. Men hun sa: "Hvis de andre gjør det, hvorfor kan ikke jeg?" Ser du hvor våre amerikanske kvinner har kommet til?

text:
I felt so bad out there at San Angelo, here not long ago, in Rome, they had a sign up: "American women, please put on some clothes before you come, to respect the dead." In a place like Rome. Shame on you. I may be burning you up, but my mama used to tell me…
When I'd take castor oil… I can't stand it yet today. Every Saturday night, a bath in an old cedar tub, and then have to take a dose of castor oil. We didn't have the right kind of food to eat and things, so she'd… I'd say, "Mama, I can't stand it." I'd hold my nose and gag. I said, "It just makes me so sick."
Says, "If it don't make you right good and sick, it don't do you no good." So maybe I'll try the same thing here, see. It get you right good and sick, then maybe it'll stir up your spiritual organs, gastronomics, so you can digest a good Holy Ghost dinner once in a while.
text_no:
For ikke lenge siden følte jeg meg virkelig ille der ute i San Angelo. I Roma hadde de nylig en plakat: "Amerikanske kvinner, vær så snill å kle dere anstendig for å vise respekt for de døde." På et sted som Roma, hva for en skam. Jeg vet dette kan være ubehagelig å høre, men min mor pleide å si...
Når jeg tok lakserolje... Jeg tåler det fortsatt ikke. Hver lørdagskveld badet jeg i et gammelt sedertrekar og måtte så ta en dose lakserolje. Vi hadde ikke all den rette maten, så hun sa... Jeg sa, "Mamma, jeg klarer det ikke." Jeg holdt meg for nesen og brakk meg. Jeg sa, "Det gjør meg så kvalm."
Hun sa, "Hvis det ikke gjør deg ordentlig kvalm, virker det ikke." Så kanskje jeg skal prøve det samme her. Hvis dette gjør deg skikkelig kvalm, kan det kanskje sette i gang dine åndelige organer, så du kan fordøye et godt måltid fra Den Hellige Ånd av og til.

text:
Notice, let me tell you, then this woman, when after a while she got to a place to where she got sick. She put it off a little while and finally she went to the doctor. Brother, it'll catch up with you as sure as anything. They found out that she had an advanced malignancy. Nothing to do but die.
The pastor of our church, Rev. Orman Neville, the Methodist church, he goes to see the woman. He said, "I'll go and see her, because I know Brother Branham knows her daddy, and I think he knows her. And I know she's living over in that kind of a life." Said, "I'm going to see her and get her straightened up," said, "because that's what he'd tell her as soon as he got there and ask him if she wants me to come over."
She walked over and he said, "Being that we belong to the same church, lady, I come to speak to you."
She said, "All right, what is it, mister."
text_no:
La meg fortelle deg hva som skjedde. Etter en tid ble denne kvinnen syk. Hun utsatte å gjøre noe med det en stund, men til slutt gikk hun til legen. Bror, du kan være sikker på at det vil innhente deg. De oppdaget at hun hadde en avansert kreftform. Det var ingenting annet å gjøre enn å forberede seg på å dø.
Pastoren i vår menighet, Rev. Orman Neville fra metodistkirken, bestemte seg for å besøke kvinnen. Han sa: "Jeg går for å se henne, fordi jeg vet at Bror Branham kjenner hennes far, og jeg tror han kjenner henne også. Og jeg vet hvilken type liv hun lever." Han sa: "Jeg skal besøke henne og få henne på rett vei, for det er det han ville fortalt henne så snart han kom dit. Jeg skal spørre henne om hun vil at jeg skal komme over."
Han gikk bort og sa: "Ettersom vi tilhører samme menighet, frue, kom jeg for å snakke med deg."
Hun svarte: "Greit, hva vil du, herr?"

text:
And he told her who he was, said, "Your father and I are very good friends, we both up there, the pastor, we went to Asbury College together." Said, "We're very good friends." Said, "I just want to ask you something." Said, "Don't you want to straighten up your life and come back to Christ?"
She said, "What?"
Said, "Straighten up your life and come back to Christ."
"Why," she said, "I'm just as good as you are."
He said, "That's not the sub… that's not the question. Don't you want to straighten up your life?"
text_no:
Han fortalte henne hvem han var og sa: "Din far og jeg er veldig gode venner. Vi var begge der oppe sammen med pastoren; vi gikk på Asbury College sammen." Så sa han: "Vi er veldig gode venner." Han fortsatte: "Jeg vil bare spørre deg om noe. Vil du ikke rette opp livet ditt og komme tilbake til Kristus?"
Hun svarte: "Hva?"
Han gjentok: "Rette opp livet ditt og komme tilbake til Kristus."
Hun sa: "Hvorfor? Jeg er like god som deg."
Han svarte: "Det er ikke poenget. Vil du ikke rette opp livet ditt?"

text:
She said, "I belong to the church, and I have since a child, I was on the cradle roll. And I'm a Christian, I've been a Christian, and when I die I'll go to heaven." She actually believed that, reasoning. And she said, "I'm just as good as you are."
He said, "You know Brother Branham?"
She said, "I've heard of him."
Said, "I want him to come over and see you." And said, "I know as soon as he come, he's going to call this thing to your hand."
Said, "What thing?"
Said, "Lady, this thing laying here, this package of cigarettes, what about that?"
She said, "Why, you fanatic, I guess you think that's wrong."
He said, "I know it's wrong!"
She said, "I never sent for you to come."
He said, "Just a minute, lady." Said, "What about that common-law husband?"
text_no:
Hun sa: "Jeg tilhører menigheten, og har gjort det siden jeg var barn, jeg var på barnerullen. Og jeg er en kristen, jeg har vært en kristen, og når jeg dør, vil jeg komme til himmelen." Hun trodde faktisk på det, resonnerte seg frem til det. Og hun sa: "Jeg er like god som deg."
Han sa: "Kjenner du Bror Branham?"
Hun svarte: "Jeg har hørt om ham."
Han sa: "Jeg vil at han skal komme og se deg." Og han sa: "Jeg vet at så snart han kommer, vil han ta tak i denne tingen i hånden din."
Hun spurte: "Hvilken ting?"
Han svarte: "Damen, denne tingen som ligger her, denne pakken med sigaretter, hva med den?"
Hun sa: "Du fanatiker, jeg antar at du synes det er galt."
Han svarte: "Jeg vet at det er galt!"
Hun sa: "Jeg ba deg aldri om å komme."
Han sa: "Et øyeblikk, dame." Og la til: "Hva med denne samboeren?"

text:
She said, "The door that you come in at, is ready for you to go out at. If I wanted somebody that had some sense to talk to me, I'd send for somebody and not you."
Said, "All right, lady, just remember."
He come back and told me when I come in. He said, "I wanted you to go there."
I said, "Just watch her at the end."
When she come to die, her backslidden pastor standing out there let her get by with such as that without telling her the truth, they was all in the room. And the first thing you know, her intellectuals begin to fail, begin to break up. She couldn't think.
text_no:
Hun sa: "Døren du kom inn gjennom, er klar for at du kan gå ut gjennom den. Hvis jeg ville ha noen med litt fornuft til å snakke med meg, ville jeg ha sendt etter noen andre, ikke deg."
Han svarte: "Greit, dame, bare husk dette."
Han kom tilbake og fortalte meg når jeg kom inn: "Jeg ville at du skulle dra dit."
Jeg sa: "Bare følg med på henne mot slutten."
Da hun skulle dø, sto hennes frafalne pastor der uten å fortelle henne sannheten, og de var alle i rommet. Før vi visste ordet av det, begynte hennes intellekt å svikte og bryte sammen. Hun klarte ikke å tenke klart.

text:
"Now the soul that sinneth, it shall die," is that right? That means to separate. Death means "separation." "He that heareth My words, believeth on Him that sent Me, has everlasting life," can't die, see. But when you grieve that soul, it goes away from you. It was gone plumb away from her, didn't talk to her no more. But when her intellects begin to break up, then nothing left here. But the soul had to come back. Well, what condition was it in? See. When it walked up it begin to rebuke her for the life she'd lived. She said, "My, God, I'm lost!"
So, the pastor said, "Oh, now, here, don't get hysterical."
She said, "I'm not hysterical." She said, "Go get Brother Neville right quick!"
Said, "Here, here, here, now, here I'll call the intern." And the doctor come in, give her a shot.
And she said, "I'm lost."
"Give her another, one doctor."
"I'm … I'm … I'm lost. I'm lost."
And the hypo stopped the confession. But that soul that she grieved, she'll have to be tormented with it as long as there's an eternity to last, see. Be careful. You take God's Word! God is in His Word, not in some man's theology, but in His Word! Do you believe it?
text_no:
"Sjelen som synder, skal dø," er det riktig? Det betyr å bli adskilt. Død betyr "adskillelse." "Den som hører Mine Ord og tror på Ham som har sendt Meg, har evig liv," og kan ikke dø. Men når du sørger en sjel, fjerner den seg fra deg. Den hadde fullstendig forlatt henne, snakket ikke mer med henne. Men da hennes forstand begynte å bryte sammen, var det ingenting igjen her. Sjeleb måtte komme tilbake. Hvilken tilstand var den i? Når den kom tilbake, begynte den å refse henne for livet hun hadde levd. Hun sa, "Min Gud, jeg er fortapt!"
Pastoren sa, "Å, nå, ro deg ned, ikke bli hysterisk."
Hun svarte, "Jeg er ikke hysterisk. Hent Bror Neville med en gang!"
Pastoren sa, "Her, her, la meg kalle på en assistent." Og legen kom inn og ga henne en sprøyte.
Hun sa, "Jeg er fortapt."
"Gi henne en til, doktor."
"Jeg... jeg... jeg er fortapt. Jeg er fortapt."
Sprøyten stoppet tilståelsen. Men den sjelen hun hadde sørget over, skal hun måtte bli pint med så lenge evigheten varer. Vær forsiktig. Ta Guds Ord! Gud er i Sitt Ord, ikke i en eller annen manns teologi, men i Hans Ord! Tror du det?

text: God is in His Son. Do you believe that? Do you believe God's in the universe? You believe God's in His Word? Now, God is in His Son. Now, quickly we'll go. God is in His Son. Now, God was in Christ reconciling the world to Himself.
text_no: Gud er i Sin Sønn. Tror du det? Tror du Gud er i universet? Tror du Gud er i Sitt Ord? Gud er i Sin Sønn. La oss gå videre. Gud var i Kristus og forsonet verden med Seg Selv.

text:
Here recently, a woman belonging to a certain church, (I'm not making no light remarks.); it's Christian Science. But I don't mean to harm you, I don't talk about anybody's religion. But look, that woman said to me, she said, "Rev. Branham, there's one fault you have that you ought not to do."
And I said, "What?"
She said, "You brag too much on Jesus."
I said, "I couldn't brag half enough for Him!"
She said, "You put too much emphasis on Him on being deity."
I said, "He was deity! He was God!"
She said, "Oh, Rev. Branham," said, "surely, a man of your caliber ought to know better."
I said, "No, Ma'am, I don't know any better, I hope I never know any different. If I do, I'm out of my mind."
She said, "Do you mean to tell me that He was divine?"
I said, "He was divine."
She said, "He couldn't be divine."
I said, "He was divine!"
She said, "Oh, He was a great teacher, He was a prophet."
text_no:
For en tid tilbake møtte jeg en kvinne fra en bestemt menighet (jeg ønsker ikke å snakke nedsettende om noen); det var Christian Science. Men jeg ønsker ikke å skade deg, og jeg snakker ikke om andres religion. Men se, denne kvinnen sa til meg: "Rev. Branham, du har en feil som du ikke burde gjøre."
Jeg spurte: "Hva?"
Hun svarte: "Du skryter for mye av Jesus."
Jeg sa: "Jeg kan ikke skryte nok av Ham!"
Hun fortsatte: "Du legger for mye vekt på at Han var guddommelig."
Jeg svarte: "Han var guddommelig! Han var Gud!"
Hun sa: "Å, Rev. Branham, en mann av din kaliber burde vite bedre."
Jeg sa: "Nei, frue, jeg vet ikke bedre, og jeg håper aldri jeg får vite noe annet. Hvis jeg gjør det, er jeg ute av fatning."
Hun spurte: "Mener du å si at Han var guddommelig?"
Jeg svarte: "Han var guddommelig."
Hun sa: "Han kunne ikke være guddommelig."
Jeg sa: "Han var guddommelig!"
Hun sa: "Å, Han var en stor lærer, Han var en profet."

text:
I said, "If He was a prophet and a great teacher, I'm lost and everybody's lost. That blood was nothing but the blood, blood of Almighty God! He was divine!"
She said, "If I prove to you by the Bible, that He was not divine, will you accept it?"
I said, "If God's Word said He wasn't divine, then I'll take back everything I said."
Said, "I'll prove it to you."
I said, "All right."
She said, "In Saint John the 11th chapter…"
"Yes."
Said, "When He was going down to the grave of Lazarus."
I said, "Yes."
"The Bible said He wept."
"Why," I said, "what's that got to do with it?"
Said, "That proved He wasn't divine. How could a divine man weep?" Said, "He could not be divine, for He wept."
I said, "You fail to see it, lady. God was in Christ reconciling the Word. He was a God-man. He was Immanuel, God was in Him!"
Said, "No, He couldn't be and weep."
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Hvis Han bare var en profet og en stor lærer, er jeg fortapt, og alle er fortapt. Det blodet var ikke annet enn blod, blodet av den Allmektige Gud! Han var guddommelig!"
Hun sa: "Hvis jeg beviser med Bibelen at Han ikke var guddommelig, vil du akseptere det?"
Jeg sa: "Hvis Guds Ord sier at Han ikke var guddommelig, vil jeg trekke tilbake alt jeg har sagt."
Hun sa: "Jeg skal bevise det for deg."
Jeg sa: "Greit."
Hun sa: "I Johannesevangeliet, kapittel 11..."
"Ja."
"Når Han var på vei til graven til Lasarus."
Jeg sa: "Ja."
"Bibelen sier at Han gråt."
"Hvorfor," sa jeg, "hva har det med saken å gjøre?"
Hun sa: "Det beviser at Han ikke var guddommelig. Hvordan kunne en guddommelig mann gråte? Han kunne ikke være guddommelig, for Han gråt."
Jeg sa: "Du forstår det ikke, frue. Gud var i Kristus og forsonte seg med Verden. Han var en Gud-mann. Han var Immanuel, Gud var i Ham!"
Hun sa: "Nei, Han kunne ikke være det og gråte."

text: I said, "Lady, I want to ask you something." I said, "He was a man when He walked to that grave a weeping. But when He stood there and straightened up them little stooped over shoulders, a man thirty looking fifty, they said. No beauty we should desire Him. He throwed those little shoulders back and said, 'I am the resurrection and life,' saith God. 'He that believeth in Me, though he were dead, yet shall he live.' Only a man… He did weep like a man, but when He straightened those little shoulders up and spoke to a man had been dead four days, corruption in his body, corruption knew its master, the soul to its creator, and a man had been dead four days stood on the ground and lived again! That was more than a man! That was more than a man! That was God in His Son."
text_no: Jeg sa, "Dame, jeg vil spørre deg om noe." Jeg sa, "Han var en mann da Han gikk gråtende til den graven. Men da Han sto der og rettet opp de litt lutende skuldrene, denne mannen som var tretti men så ut som femti, som de sa, uten skjønnhet vi skulle begjære Ham. Han kastet de små skuldrene tilbake og sa: 'Jeg er oppstandelsen og livet,' sier Gud. 'Den som tror på Meg, om han enn dør, skal han leve.' Han gråt som en mann, men da Han rettet opp de små skuldrene og talte til en mann som hadde vært død i fire dager, kjente forråtnelsen sin herre, sjelen sin skaper, og en mann som hadde vært død i fire dager sto opp og levde igjen! Det var mer enn en mann! Det var mer enn en mann! Det var Gud i Sin Sønn."

text:
When He come down off the hill that night, hungry, looking around over that tree for some fruit and couldn't find any, He was hungry like a man. That's right. He was a man then. But when He took five biscuits and two little fishes and fed five thousand, that was more than a man! That was God in His Son. Truly.
He was a man. When He laid out there on that little ship that night, looked like a bottle stopper, tossed about, and Him so tired, virtue had gone out of Him, till the waves didn't wake Him up. Ten thousand devils of the sea swore they'd drown Him, Him laying back there, back in that boat and it pitching every way and Him laying on a pillow. That was a man laying there asleep, sure. But when He woke up, put His foot upon the brail of the boat, looked up and said, "Peace be still!" And the winds and waves obeyed Him. That was more than a man! That was the divine Holy Jehovah God speaking from His Son, Christ Jesus. Right.
text_no:
Da Han kom ned fra fjellet den kvelden, sulten og lette etter frukt på treet uten å finne noe, var Han sulten som et menneske. Det stemmer, Han var et menneske da. Men da Han tok fem brød og to små fisker og mettet fem tusen, var det mer enn et menneske! Det var Gud i Sin Sønn. Helt riktig.
Han var et menneske. Da Han lå der på den lille båten den natten, som en kork som ble kastet hit og dit, og var så utmattet at bølgene ikke vekket Ham, hadde kraft gått ut av Ham. Ti tusen djevler i havet sverget at de skulle drukne Ham, der Han lå bak i båten som ble kastet rundt, og sov på en pute. Det var et menneske som lå der og sov, helt klart. Men da Han våknet, satte foten på båtripa, så opp og sa: "Fred, vær stille!" Og vindene og bølgene adlød Ham. Det var mer enn et menneske! Det var den guddommelige, hellige Jehova Gud som talte gjennom Sin Sønn, Kristus Jesus. Helt riktig.

text:
When He died on the cross, calling for mercy, that was a man. That was a man when He died. But when He broke the seals on Easter morning and rose up, He proved He was God when He come up from the grave.
One of the poets said:
Living, He loved me; dying, He saved me;
Buried, He carried my sins far away;
Rising, He justified freely forever:
Some day He's coming---oh, glorious day!
God was in His Son. You believe it? Sure, He was. God is in His universe. God is in His Word. God is in His Son. Now, God's in His people too. Amen. You believe it?
text_no:
Da Han døde på korset og bad om nåde, var det et menneske. Det var et menneske som døde der. Men da Han brøt seglene på påskemorgen og stod opp, viste Han at Han var Gud ved å stå opp fra graven.
En av poetene sa:
Levde, Han elsket meg; døde, Han frelste meg;
Begravet, Han bar mine synder langt bort;
Oppstått, Han rettferdiggjorde meg for alltid:
En dag kommer Han---oh, herlige dag!
Gud var i Sin Sønn. Tror du det? Ja, det gjør du. Gud er i sitt univers. Gud er i Sitt Ord. Gud er i Sin Sønn. Og nå er Gud også i Sitt folk. Amen. Tror du det?

text: A cowardly little bunch sneaking around the corner, locked themselves in an upper room, had been with Him for three years and six months. When they walked up there they were scared of the Jews. Jesus had told them, said, "A little while, the world won't see Me no more; yet I will be with you to the end of the world."
text_no: En ynkelig liten gruppe som snek seg rundt hjørnet og låste seg inne i et øvre rom, hadde vært med Ham i tre år og seks måneder. Da de gikk dit, var de redde for jødene. Jesus hadde sagt til dem: "En liten stund, og verden skal ikke se Meg mer; men Jeg skal være med dere til verdens ende."

text: And when they locked themselves up, and waiting for the promise, they were a bunch of cowards. They had God on the outside, they knowed all the creeds and the formulas and everything, but all of a sudden God came down like a rushing mighty wind, filled all the house where they were sitting! Something took place! Out through the doors and windows they went into the street, staggering under the mighty impact of the Holy Ghost. They went around the country healing their sick and raising the dead, and casting out devils. And signs and wonders begin to follow them. And a living God, the King of Israel, was in His people, in the tent. Amen.
text_no: Da de hadde låst seg inne og ventet på løftet, var de en gjeng feiginger. De hadde Gud på utsiden, kjente alle trosbekjennelsene og formlene, men plutselig kom Gud ned som et mektig, susende vindpust og fylte hele huset hvor de satt! Noe skjedde! De strømmet ut gjennom dører og vinduer, sjanglet under den mektige kraften fra Den Hellige Ånd. De gikk rundt i landet, helbredet de syke, oppvakte de døde og drev ut onde ånder. Tegn og under begynte å følge dem. En levende Gud, Israels Konge, var blant Sitt folk, i teltet. Amen.

text:
God is in His people, showing Himself alive. God's here this afternoon. God's in His people. You believe it? Sure He is. Is He in His universe? Is He in His Word? Is He in His Son? And He's in His people. Do you know what? The Bible said you're gods because you're a part of God, you're sons and daughters of God. When your theology is left, all of your mental things here is not looking at anything else but the Word of God, and the Holy Ghost comes down here to bear witness, you are a part of God.
I'm a part of Charles Branham because I'm his son. You're a part of God because you're His son, born of His Spirit, washed in His blood. Hallelujah! Yes, you are, you're a part of Him. And you become a creator. Do you know that? Did you know…
text_no:
Gud er i Sitt folk og viser Seg levende. Gud er her i ettermiddag. Gud er i Sitt folk. Tror du det? Selvfølgelig er Han det. Er Han i Sitt univers? Er Han i Sitt Ord? Er Han i Sin Sønn? Og Han er i Sitt folk. Vet du hva? Bibelen sier at dere er guder fordi dere er en del av Gud, dere er Guds sønner og døtre. Når teologien din legger alt av mentale ting til side og fokuserer kun på Guds Ord, og Den Hellige Ånd kommer ned her for å vitne, da er du en del av Gud.
Jeg er en del av Charles Branham fordi jeg er hans sønn. Du er en del av Gud fordi du er Hans sønn, født av Hans Ånd, renset i Hans blod. Halleluja! Ja, det er du, du er en del av Ham. Og du blir en skaper. Visste du det? Visste du...

text:
Let me give you a little speck of light just in a minute now. Just a few minutes now before we start the prayer line. You are a creator yourself, a miniature creator. Listen. Did you ever see people that was nice people, though you just couldn't be around them hardly. Something about them you just couldn't stand? Did you ever see people that you just love to be around? Did you ever see them kind of people? That's the atmosphere that they create themselves.
And let me tell you today, my dear friends: the greatest thing there is this side of heaven is l-o-v-e, divine love. God is love. You read in the book when the maniac run out to kill me on the platform that night. I didn't hate the man, I loved him, sure, felt sorry for him. That's what slew him on the platform. That's what slew those witch-doctors standing there that day. Thinking human beings that God died for, standing there trying to throw spells on me, witches like that. Standing there before Him and you see them fall to the ground, throw their head down like this and give their hearts to Christ. It was love that did it! Love is the mightiest force there is. Even phileo love will make a mother jump through a fire to grab her burning baby. She won't think of herself.
text_no:
La meg gi deg en liten lysglimt nå med en gang. Bare noen minutter før vi starter bønnekøen. Du er en skaper selv, en miniatyrskaper. Hør her. Har du noen gang møtt mennesker som var hyggelige, men som du nesten ikke klarte å være rundt? Det var noe ved dem som du bare ikke kunne tåle. Har du møtt mennesker du bare elsker å være rundt? Har du sett slike mennesker? Det er atmosfæren de skaper selv.
La meg fortelle deg, kjære venner: det største som finnes utenom himmelen er k-j-æ-r-l-i-g-h-e-t, guddommelig kjærlighet. Gud er kjærlighet. Du leser i Skriften om natten da den gale løp ut for å drepe meg på plattformen. Jeg hatet ikke mannen, jeg elsket ham. Jeg følte medlidenhet med ham. Det var det som stoppet ham på plattformen. Det var det samme som stoppet heksedoktorene som stod der den dagen. De var tenkende mennesker som Gud døde for, som stod der og prøvde å kaste forbannelser på meg, hekser som dem. Stod der foran Ham, og du ser dem falle til bakken, bøye hodet og gi sine hjerter til Kristus. Det var kjærlighet som gjorde det! Kjærlighet er den mektigste kraften som finnes. Selv phileo-kjærlighet vil få en mor til å hoppe gjennom flammer for å redde sitt brennende barn. Hun vil ikke tenke på seg selv.

text: Think of a woman one time, standing… A young girl had been away to college and she come back. And when she did, she was standing out by the train station, the train pulled in and everybody was hollering "Hurrah!" And she brought her snicklefritz girl with her from school. And her mother was standing out there, and the little snicklefritz girl said, "Hey, who is that horrible looking wretch there, look at the way she looks."
text_no: Tenk på en kvinne en gang, som stod der... En ung jente hadde vært på college og kom tilbake. Da hun gjorde det, stod hun ved togstasjonen. Toget kom inn og alle ropte "Hurra!" Hun hadde med seg sin snicklefritz-venninne fra skolen. Moren hennes stod der, og den lille snicklefritz-venninnen sa: "Hei, hvem er den forferdelige skapningen der? Se hvordan hun ser ut."

text:
And the girl, being a little smarter, what she'd learned in school, she was ashamed of her mother. And when she got off the platform, her mother said, "Darling, I'm so glad to see you." She turned her back and walked away because her little friend had done that. It happened to be, the conductor knowed the story, he said, "Come here, girl." He grabbed her by the shoulder, said, "Ain't you ashamed of yourself, denying your own mother?"
She said, "I don't know her." 'Cause her…
She said, "Martha, is this your mama?"
Said, "No, I don't even know her."
text_no:
Jenta, som var litt smartere etter hva hun hadde lært på skolen, skammet seg over moren sin. Da hun gikk av perrongen, sa moren: "Kjære, jeg er så glad for å se deg." Jenta snudde ryggen til og gikk bort fordi venninnen hennes hadde gjort det samme. Konduktøren, som kjente til historien, ropte: "Kom hit, jente." Han tok tak i skulderen hennes og sa: "Er du ikke flau over deg selv, som nekter for din egen mor?"
Hun svarte: "Jeg kjenner henne ikke." 
Han spurte: "Martha, er dette din mor?"
Hun svarte: "Nei, jeg kjenner henne ikke engang."

text:
Conductor said, "Be ashamed of you." Said, "Look here, girl, I want to tell you something." And the crowd begin to listen. Said, "You're a beautiful woman today, that's right." Said, "You are. But," said, "your mother was a lot more beautiful woman than you were, or ever will be."
And said, "One day when she was out in the back yard hanging up the clothes," said, "I lived on the same block." And said, "The house caught fire, you was a little baby, upstairs in your crib. And when the fire engine came and the mother seen it stop in front of her house---where the neighbors had called---away she went, but the stairs was just about ready to fall in." He said "That mother, not thinking for her, jumped through those blazes and the fireman grabbing after her, she went through the blazes anyhow. And she ran in there and grabbed you off the bed. Jerked the clothes ever she had on her body and rolled them around your face and things. And she braved that fire blaze right out back through."
Said, "It burned, it scorched her, it wilted her down. And today because you're pretty, she was made ugly. And you mean to tell me, you'd deny that woman that saved your life and your beauty, that you're ashamed about now because of your mother, that you'd do that?"
text_no:
Konduktøren sa: "Du burde skamme deg." Han fortsatte: "Hør her, jente, jeg vil fortelle deg noe." Folkemengden begynte å lytte. "Du er en vakker kvinne i dag, det stemmer," sa han. "Men," la han til, "moren din var mye vakrere enn du er, eller noen gang vil bli."
Han fortalte videre: "En dag da hun var ute i bakgården og hengte opp klær, bodde jeg på samme kvartal. Huset tok fyr, og du var en liten baby, oppe i krybben din. Da brannbilen kom, og naboene hadde varslet, løp moren din til huset. Trappene var på nippet til å kollapse. Uten å tenke på seg selv, stupte hun gjennom flammene. Brannmennene prøvde å stoppe henne, men hun fortsatte. Hun løp inn og tok deg fra sengen. Hun rev av de klærne hun hadde på seg og pakket dem rundt ansiktet og kroppen din. Deretter kjempet hun seg gjennom flammehavet igjen."
Han sa: "Det brant og svidde henne. Hun ble vansiret. I dag, fordi du er vakker, ble hun gjort stygg. Betyr det at du nå, fordi du skammer deg over moren din, ville fornekte kvinnen som reddet livet ditt og din skjønnhet? At du virkelig ville gjøre det?"

text: When that story was told to me, I thought, "O God, how could I, though the old rugged cross so despised by the world, when I see that Christ became sin that I by His righteousness might become a part of Him." How much do I care they call me a holy-roller. How much do I care they call me a divine healer. Whatever they want to, He came down and was made ugly and sinful, that by His righteousness I might have a home in heaven and a right to be the son of the living God, and to believe on Him. How it is, He became me that I by His grace, might become Him. He taken my place as a sinner, as me as a sinner might take His place as righteousness. How could we deny Him? Sure.
text_no: Da jeg hørte den fortellingen, tenkte jeg: "O Gud, hvordan kunne jeg, gjennom det gamle, forakte kors, når jeg ser at Kristus ble til synd slik at jeg ved Hans rettferdighet kunne bli en del av Ham." Hvor mye bryr jeg meg om at de kaller meg en hellig-ruller? Hvor mye bryr jeg meg om at de kaller meg en guddommelig helbreder? De kan si hva de vil; Han kom ned og ble gjort stygg og syndig, slik at jeg ved Hans rettferdighet kunne få et hjem i himmelen og rett til å være sønn av den levende Gud og tro på Ham. Hvordan er det mulig at Han ble meg, slik at jeg ved Hans nåde kunne bli Ham? Han tok min plass som synder, slik at jeg som synder kunne ta Hans plass som rettferdig. Hvordan kunne vi nekte Ham? Absolutt.

text:
Create around you love. Who could deny such great love? Listen just a moment what a great love is. Love will conquer, where hate and malice, and creed, debate, and fuss, will drive away. Love will conquer. Whew. I want to tell you something now. Here, give you some of my heart, the inside life, just a minute, or two, and then we're going to start the prayer line. We got just about maybe six or eight minutes. Look just a minute.
Here a few days ago, I was showing someone… The house had been full all the day, people everywhere. The babies couldn't even eat, nothing. Finally when the house got emptied up, and somebody come in and just had the people to go, coming from, not one place, all over the world. Signs on the gates everywhere else, "Don't come in," they come anyhow. You can't blame them, they're human beings, wanting help. I'm not there, but just a few hours at a time, my family's strange to me almost.
text_no:
Skap kjærlighet rundt deg. Hvem kan benekte en slik stor kjærlighet? Lytt et øyeblikk til hva ekte kjærlighet er. Kjærlighet vil seire der hat, ondskap, doktriner, debatt og krangel driver bort. Kjærlighet vil seire. Puh. Jeg vil dele noe med dere nå. Jeg vil gi dere en bit av mitt hjerte, mitt indre liv, bare et minutt eller to, og deretter skal vi starte bønnekøen. Vi har omtrent seks eller åtte minutter. Se her et øyeblikk.
For noen dager siden viste jeg noe til noen… Huset hadde vært fullt hele dagen, med folk overalt. Babyene kunne ikke engang spise, ingenting. Da huset endelig ble tømt, kom noen inn og fikk folk til å gå, de kom fra ikke bare ett sted, men fra hele verden. Skilt på portene overalt sa "Ikke kom inn," men de kom likevel. Du kan ikke klandre dem, de er mennesker som ønsker hjelp. Jeg er der bare noen få timer av gangen, og min familie er nesten fremmed for meg.

text:
I went in, and there stood my dear beloved wife in a corner crying. There stood Sarah and Rebekah fighting over some blocks in the next room. Little Joseph screaming at the top of his voice. The last one had left. Billy and them had come up and got them all out, Brother Woods and my neighbors and them. So, I sat there. I thought, "What is all nervous and upset." I thought, "Heavenly Father, we got to make this situation here different."
So, I walked over, she said, "Billy, honey, what am I going to do?" Said, "I'm losing my mind; all day long, that pounding, and everybody in here, people saying, 'If you don't come to this city, they done got you advertised and you're going to be cursed by God if you don't.'"
I said, "Honey, that's fanatics. If God wants me to go anywhere, He will tell me." And I said, "I'm on speaking terms with Him." And I said, "If He wants me to go… Don't pay no attention to them things." I said, "We have fanaticism in everything and saying this, that, and the other."
text_no:
Jeg gikk inn, og der sto min kjære kone i et hjørne og gråt. Sarah og Rebekah kranglet over noen klosser i neste rom. Lille Joseph skrek av full hals. Den siste personen hadde dratt. Billy og de andre hadde kommet og fått alle ut, inkludert Bror Woods og mine naboer. Så jeg satte meg ned og tenkte, "Hva er det som er så nervøst og opprørt her." Jeg ba, "Himmelske Far, vi må endre på denne situasjonen."
Jeg gikk bort til henne, og hun sa, "Billy, kjære, hva skal jeg gjøre? Jeg mister forstanden; hele dagen er det banking, og alle mennesker som er her, sier at 'Hvis du ikke kommer til denne byen, har de allerede annonsert deg, og du vil bli forbannet av Gud hvis du ikke kommer.'"
Jeg svarte, "Kjære, det er fanatikere. Hvis Gud vil at jeg skal dra et sted, vil Han fortelle meg det." Jeg fortsatte, "Jeg er i samtale med Ham. Hvis Han vil at jeg skal dra… Ikke bry deg om slike ting." Jeg la til, "Det finnes fanatisme i alt, og folk sier ting uten grunn."

text:
And she was crying, and I got talking to her, I said, "But you know, honey, I seen the prettiest little dress in a certain city, you know." And the first thing you know she was peeling potatoes, she was all quietened down. When she quietened the children quietened, Joseph quietened; all right. See, you have to change the situation. You can do it.
Let me tell you something. This is a little inside, you may judge me wrong after this. When I was game warden, I've had this to happen many times. You heard me speak about being in the mountains, see, out in the mountains with wild life. Now, there's something about it. See, you have to know the Creator.
text_no:
Hun gråt, og jeg begynte å snakke med henne. Jeg sa: "Vet du, kjære, jeg så den vakreste lille kjolen i en bestemt by." Før jeg visste ordet av det, sto hun der og skrelte poteter, og hun var helt roet ned. Når hun roet seg, roet også barna seg, og Josef roet seg også. Ser du, man må endre situasjonen. Du kan gjøre det.
La meg fortelle deg noe. Dette er litt personlig, og du kan dømme meg feil etter dette. Da jeg var viltvokter, har dette hendt mange ganger. Du har hørt meg snakke om å være i fjellene, ikke sant, ute i naturen med dyrelivet. Det er noe spesielt med det. Du må kjenne Skaperen.

text: Now, wild animals will hurt you if you're afraid of them. But if you're not afraid of them, and you love them, they won't hurt you. You can't bluff it now, you got to really mean it. I've walked face to face with grizzly bears, African lions and everything else, see. That's right. They won't hurt you if you're not afraid. But there's something about you, your body puts out a … your nerves puts out an odor. Did you ever see a dog? He will… [Brother Branham imitates sniffing.] Don't try to bluff him, he knows whether you're afraid or not. He does. If you're afraid, he'll bite you. If you're not afraid, I've never seen one yet, when I walk in pet him, walk on through the yard, see. If you're not afraid of them, they know it.
text_no: Ville dyr vil skade deg hvis du er redd dem. Men hvis du ikke er redd og du elsker dem, vil de ikke skade deg. Du kan ikke late som, du må virkelig mene det. Jeg har møtt grizzlybjørner, afrikanske løver og mange andre dyr ansikt til ansikt. De vil ikke skade deg hvis du ikke er redd. Kroppen din utstråler noe, nervene dine sender ut en lukt. Har du noen gang sett en hund? Han vil… [Bror Branham etterligner sniffing.] Ikke prøv å lure ham, han vet om du er redd eller ikke. Hvis du er redd, vil han bite deg. Hvis du ikke er redd, har jeg aldri sett en som biter deg når jeg klapper den og går gjennom hagen. Hvis du ikke er redd dem, vet de det.

text:
Here, one day I was down at the bottom of the hill, turning some fish loose; I was in the conservation. We're supposed to wear a pistol, but I didn't want no pistol. If I talked to a man, I believe it'd made a better guy out of him than if I could arrest him anyhow.
So then, we've got to turn loose some fish. And I remembered, across the hill, I had an old friend over there was dying … or, very sick. I thought, "I'll walk over and see him." I just closed up the truck door and started walking across the field, walked up over it. It ain't like here, there's just some little shrubs out in the field. And when I walked right out there and got in the middle of the field, behind those shrubs I didn't notice, but there…
text_no:
En dag var jeg nede ved bunnen av bakken og slapp ut noen fisk. Jeg jobbet i naturvernet. Vi skulle egentlig bære pistol, men jeg ønsket ikke å bære en. Jeg tror at om jeg snakket med en mann, ville det ha hatt en bedre effekt på ham enn å arrestere ham.
Så skulle vi slippe ut noen fisk. Jeg husket at jeg hadde en gammel venn over bakken som var døende eller veldig syk. Jeg tenkte: "Jeg går over og ser til ham." Jeg lukket lastebilsdøren og begynte å gå over jordet. Det er ikke som her, det var bare noen små busker ute på feltet. Da jeg gikk ut midt på jordet, la jeg ikke merke til buskene bak meg, men der...

text: About six weeks before then a great big Durham bull had killed a colored man down there. He's a killer---longhorn. He's down at the Burk's farm, he killed him. So, this was up at another farm. They'd sold him, because he was a fine breeder bull. But when he was… That bull raised right up in the field. I forgot about him being in there. And there he was not over twenty spaces from me. He raised up and looked at me, he let out a roar and throwed them horns in the ground like that. I looked back to the fence, I couldn't outrun him. There was no tree to get into. What could I do? I was in the same spot like I was with that maniac you was reading in the book. What could I do?
text_no: Omtrent seks uker før det hadde en stor Durham-okse drept en farget mann der nede. Han er en drapsokse—en langhorn. Han er nede på Burks gård, og han drepte mannen der. Så, dette var på en annen gård. De hadde solgt ham, fordi han var en utmerket avlsokse. Men da han var... Den oksen reiste seg i feltet. Jeg hadde glemt at han var der inne. Og der var han, ikke mer enn tjue skritt fra meg. Han reiste seg og så på meg, brølte og kastet hornene i bakken. Jeg så tilbake mot gjerdet; jeg kunne ikke løpe fra ham. Det var ingen tre å klatre i. Hva kunne jeg gjøre? Jeg var i samme situasjon som jeg var med den galningen du leste om i boken. Hva kunne jeg gjøre?

text:
Now listen, you can't make it, it has to happen. Now listen close. But somehow another, something happened in my heart: Instead of being scared, I loved him. That sounds juvenile, but that's true. I thought, "Poor fellow, he was just sleeping; I disturbed him."
And here he come, throwing his horns down like that, and he hit on his knees and throwed his horns again, twisting his tail, and here he come. Well, instead of being afraid, I was no more afraid of that bull than I am of my brethren sitting here on the platform. He come right to… If you could ever get that! Wish I could live in that all the time.
text_no:
Hør her, det er ikke noe du kan få til selv, det må skje. Hør nøye. Men på en eller annen måte skjedde det noe i hjertet mitt: I stedet for å være redd, elsket jeg ham. Det høres barnslig ut, men det er sant. Jeg tenkte, "Stakkars fyr, han sov bare; jeg forstyrret ham."
Så kom han mot meg, kastet hornene ned sånn og traff med knærne, kastet hornene igjen, vred på halen, og her kom han. Men i stedet for å være redd, var jeg ikke mer redd for den oksen enn jeg er for mine brødre som sitter her på plattformen. Han kom rett mot... Hvis du noen gang kunne forstå det! Skulle ønske jeg kunne leve slik hele tiden.

text:
But here he come. He come right straight to me and I said, "Now, I'm sorry I disturbed you; you are a creation of God. I am a servant of the Creator, Jesus. I'm on my road over to see His servant that's sick. I must go pray for him. I'm sorry I disturbed you. Now in the name of Jesus Christ, go back and lay down; I won't bother you. We'll go on by."
And that bull, with his head down, here he come making a charge. Well, we'll have to meet… I don't know what you think about it, but I can only state facts. That bull got within about five foot of me, just as … and I was no more scared. I thought, "Poor fellow, I'm sorry I disturbed you." I come on his ground. And he run right straight to me and he stopped, and he looked so depleted; he looked this way, looked that way, turned around, walked over and laid down. I stood there, walked by him within five foot of him like that; he looked up at me. Walked over and got through the fence, then I began shaking.
What was it? God. He'd have killed me. The same God that could stop a lion, sent that Angel of fire, that light standing between Daniel, the animal scared of light. There that Angel standing there, sure. What happened? The lion went over and laid down.
The same God that lived in Daniel's day is the same God today. He could stop a lion, could stop the bull.
text_no:
Men der kom han. Han kom rett mot meg, og jeg sa, "Nå, jeg beklager at jeg forstyrret deg; du er en skapning av Gud. Jeg er en tjener av Skaperen, Jesus. Jeg er på vei for å se Hans tjener som er syk. Jeg må gå og be for ham. Jeg beklager at jeg forstyrret deg. Nå, i Jesu Kristi navn, gå tilbake og legg deg ned; jeg vil ikke forstyrre deg. Vi går videre."
Og oksen, med hodet ned, kom mot meg i fullt angrep. Vel, vi måtte møtes… Jeg vet ikke hva du tenker om det, men jeg kan bare fortelle fakta. Oksen kom innen omtrent fem fot fra meg, og jeg var ikke redd. Jeg tenkte, "Stakkars fyr, jeg beklager at jeg forstyrret deg." Jeg hadde trengt inn på hans territorium. Han løp rett mot meg, stoppet, og så utmattet ut; han så seg rundt, snudde seg, gikk bort og la seg ned. Jeg stod der, gikk forbi ham, innen fem fot av ham; han så opp på meg. Jeg gikk over og passerte gjennom gjerdet, og da begynte jeg å skjelve.
Hva var det? Gud. Han ville ha drept meg. Den samme Gud som kunne stoppe en løve, sendte den Ildens Engel, det lyset som stod mellom Daniel, dyret redde lyset. Der sto den Engelen, helt sikkert. Hva skjedde? Løven gikk bort og la seg ned.
Den samme Gud som levde på Daniels tid, er den samme Gud i dag. Han som kunne stoppe en løve, kunne stoppe oksen.

text:
My grass was growing up in the yard last summer. I was mowing out there, and I'd have to make a couple of rounds then change my overalls, come in pray for the sick, and go back out. It'd grow up in the front yard before I get to the back. Now listen.
I got back in the back yard, I took off my shirt: nobody back there; it's way back behind the place. And I was just a pushing as hard as I could, and this little old power mower, and it bang, bang, bang. I forgot about a big nest of hornets hanging in the corner. And I rammed this lawn mower into that, and in a minute I was covered over with great… You know what hornets are: great big fellows. They'll knock … they'll kill you.
text_no:
I fjor sommer vokste gresset i hagen som bare det. Jeg drev og klippet plenen, men måtte stadig skifte kjeledress, komme inn for å be for de syke, og så gå ut igjen. Gresset rakk å vokse opp i forhaven før jeg kom til bakhagen. Nå, lytt.
Da jeg kom til bakhagen, tok jeg av meg skjorten; det var jo ingen der bak, langt unna huset. Jeg presset på så hardt jeg kunne med den lille gamle gressklipperen mens den gikk bang, bang, bang. Jeg hadde glemt et stort vepsebol som hang i hjørnet. Jeg kjørte gressklipperen rett inn i det, og på et øyeblikk var jeg dekket med svære… Dere vet hva veps er: store kraftige typer. De kan slå til … de kan drepe deg.

text:
And here I was with no shirt on, and covered over with hornets, around like that. Now instead… Something happened again. Instead of being afraid, I wasn't afraid. And I thought, "Now I disturbed them; isn't that a shame?" I said, "Now little creatures of God, I am the servant of God, I am mowing this grass, I've got to hurry 'cause God's sick children is coming in to be prayed for, and I got to hurry. I'm sorry I disturbed you; I'm sorry. Now in Jesus' name you go back to your nest. I won't bother you."
And God who is my solemn judge, with this Bible laying here, them hornets whirled around me, I just stood there, they made one single file like that, they went right straight back into the nest, and that was all of it. I seen it happen many times.
text_no:
Der sto jeg uten skjorte, dekket av veps. Men i stedet for å bli redd, følte jeg ingen frykt. Jeg tenkte: "Nå har jeg forstyrret dem; er ikke det synd?" Jeg sa: "Små skapninger av Gud, jeg er en tjener for Gud. Jeg klipper dette gresset raskt, for Guds syke barn kommer for å bli bedt for, og jeg må skynde meg. Jeg beklager at jeg forstyrret dere. Nå, i Jesu navn, gå tilbake til reiret deres. Jeg skal ikke plage dere mer."
Og Gud, som er min høyeste dommer, med denne Bibelen her, kan bekrefte at vepsene svirret rundt meg, og jeg sto bare rolig. De dannet en enkelt file og gikk rett tilbake til reiret. Det var alt. Jeg har sett det skje mange ganger.

text:
Here, Leo and Gene---they're sitting right here behind me now, they're kind of student ministers. They were sitting on the porch last summer---then we're closing. About ten o'clock in the morning, wasn't it, boys? Around ten o'clock in the morning, sitting there, I was talking to them about a colored girl; her picture's in the paper. She took her baby---a lovely looking woman---and took her baby and smothered it in a blanket and took it out on the bridge in a taxi cab and dropped it off in the water. They caught her. And I said, "That was not a mother; that's a female. Not a mother; a mother wouldn't do that."
And here come the milkman up bringing the milk. Mr. Woods (He will be with me the next meeting.) His wife's a veterinarian; their boy had been cripple with a drawed up leg. He was a Jehovah Witness, she was a Methodist. They brought him to the meeting, was sitting way back. Hundreds … thousands of people in there at… What was that name, Paul? Where? No. To where David Woods was healed. No it's up here, where that big lake is, oh, I'll get it in a few minutes. Anyhow, it's up here on the Great Lakes. And he was sitting way back in the meeting. And the Holy Spirit come over and said, "You're a contractor, you're a Jehovah Witness, your wife there is a Methodist, she has a tumor sticking out. You got a boy with you that's got a drawed up leg from polio, but 'Thus saith the Lord,' you're healed," see.
text_no:
Leo og Gene sitter rett bak meg nå; de er slags studentforkynnere. De satt på verandaen i fjor sommer. Vi skal snart avslutte. Klokken var rundt ti om morgenen, ikke sant, gutter? Cirka ti om morgenen. Jeg snakket med dem om en farget jente; bildet hennes stod i avisen. Hun tok barnet sitt---en vakker kvinne---pakket det inn i et teppe, tok en drosje til en bro og kastet barnet i vannet. De fanget henne. Jeg sa, "Det var ikke en mor; det var en kvinnelig person. Ikke en mor; en mor ville ikke gjort det."
Melkemannen kom med melken. Mr. Woods (Han blir med meg på neste møte.) Hans kone er veterinær; deres sønn hadde vært krøpling med et sammenkrøpet ben. Han var Jehovas vitne, hun var metodist. De tok ham med til møtet, satt langt bak. Hundrevis … tusenvis av mennesker var der. Hva var navnet, Paul? Hvor? Nei. Hvor David Woods ble helbredet. Nei, det er her oppe, der den store innsjøen er. Jeg kommer på det snart. Uansett, det er her oppe ved De store sjøene. Han satt langt bak i møtet. Og Den Hellige Ånd kom over og sa, "Du er en entreprenør, du er et Jehovas vitne, din kone der er metodist, hun har en utstående svulst. Du har en gutt med et sammenkrøpet ben fra polio, men 'Så sier Herren,' dere er helbredet," ser du.

text:
And she sat there a little bit, they begin to look. She said, "Banks," to her husband, (if you're at the next meeting, you hear her testify) said, "Looky here." Said, "David, stand up." There he was just as normal; he's my boy's buddy here now. So, he quit contracting, she quit the veterinary, they've moved over and live door-neighbors to me.
They were standing in the yard, and Gene and Leo. And down the road come a possum. You have possums up here, don't you? Possums? Now anyone knows that knows conservation, a possum is blind in the daytime, he's a night prowler. Here he come down the road, come past three or four houses that didn't have no fences, turned right in mine with a fence, a rock wall, and come right up to meet me. I said, "There comes a possum; he's got rabies." I said, "I'd better go out and stop him."
text_no:
Hun satt der en stund, og de begynte å se på ham. Hun sa: "Banks," til mannen sin, (hvis du er på neste møte, hører du henne vitne) sa: "Se her." Sa: "David, reis deg." Der sto han helt normal; han er nå min sønns kamerat. De sluttet med entreprenørvirksomheten, hun sluttet som veterinær, og de har flyttet og bor nå i nabohuset til meg.
De sto i hagen, og Gene og Leo var der også. Nedover veien kom en opossum. Dere har opossumer her, ikke sant? Alle som kjenner til naturvern, vet at en opossum er blind på dagtid; den er en nattjeger. Her kom den nedover veien, gikk forbi tre eller fire hus uten gjerder, svingte rett inn i min hage som hadde et gjerde og en steinmur, og kom rett mot meg. Jeg sa: "Der kommer en opossum; den har rabies." Jeg sa: "Jeg bør gå ut og stoppe den."

text:
Mr. Woods had been raking in the yard, so I took the rake and laid over it. I called Gene and Leo out there, and they all come out and was looking at the possum. I said, "Look at him." And I happened to notice, he didn't have rabies, but his left shoulder here, was all chewed up, the dogs had got a hold of it. Now this is bad to say, but gangrene had set in and maggots had blowed it and that leg was broke to pieces, and dragging it like this. I said, "Look at that poor thing." I said, "That's what's causing it to be that way."
And usually they'll play possum when you touch them, but she was just a fighting to get up from there. And as I noticed, she… Now there's only two animals that has pockets, that's a kangaroo and a possum---where they pack their little ones. And her nerves let down and that little pocket come open, and she had nine little naked babies about that long---little possums. And I said, "Look at there boys." I said, "Come here. That possum is more of a mother than that colored girl was." I said, "That colored girl with a healthy baby, so she could keep running around, she drowned her baby. And this old mother possum's got about twenty minutes longer, or half hour, at the most, to live, and she'll spend that twenty minutes fighting for her babies." Yes sir. I said, "God's with that possum." And I said, "You look at there; ain't that something?"
text_no:
Mr. Woods hadde raket i hagen, så jeg tok riva og la den til side. Jeg ropte på Gene og Leo, og de kom ut og så på opossumen. Jeg sa: "Se på den." Jeg la merke til at den ikke hadde rabies, men at dens venstre skulder var helt opprevet; hundene hadde tatt tak i den. Nå er dette vondt å si, men koldbrann hadde satt seg, og markene hadde infisert såret, og benet var brukket i biter og ble dratt etter seg. Jeg sa: "Se på det stakkars dyret." Jeg sa: "Det er derfor den er slik."
